name: Developer Product Minimal
firstViewport: Sharp headline, terse product explanation, command/code or product preview, direct docs/signup action.
layoutRules: Prioritize product proof over decoration.
Use code-native UI, docs links, and crisp grid alignment.
typography: Tight, highly legible, technical, with controlled heading scale.
risks: Can feel generic if the product preview is weak.

name: Operational SaaS Console
avoidFor: Consumer launches that need emotional storytelling
firstViewport: Product UI is the hero: sidebar, rows, status, selected state, and clear primary action.
layoutRules: Favor app shell, compact rows, and real states.
Do not turn dense workflows into card-only marketing sections.
typography: Product UI typography with compact labels and strong information hierarchy.
risks: May feel too restrained if the brief expects a big launch moment.

name: Enterprise Trust Solution
avoidFor: Playful consumer products or experimental portfolios
firstViewport: Clear value proposition, trust proof, architecture or workflow diagram, sales CTA.
layoutRules: Use proof, governance, and flow diagrams.
Keep visuals polished but conservative.
typography: Executive-readable hierarchy with measured display type and clean body copy.
risks: Can become bland if no specific proof points are included.

name: Data Dashboard Command Center
firstViewport: Dense but ordered shell with metric summary, chart, table, filters, and selected state.
layoutRules: Use tables and charts where the workflow needs them.
Make density scannable, not decorative.
typography: Compact UI typography with strong numeric emphasis.
risks: Easy to overfill with fake metrics; seed data must feel domain-specific.

name: Editorial Technical Docs
firstViewport: Typographic hero, concise explanation, docs entry point, architecture or code sample.
layoutRules: Let typography and information architecture lead.
Use diagrams and examples instead of generic feature cards.
typography: Editorial hierarchy with readable prose and code-friendly monospace accents.
risks: Can undersell commercial value if no product outcome is shown.

name: Motion Rich Launch
firstViewport: Strong visual hook, animated proof or product reveal, short CTA path.
layoutRules: Use motion as hierarchy, not filler.
Keep core copy short and readable.
typography: Display-forward but readable, with restrained UI text.
risks: Can become gimmicky if every section animates.

name: Consumer Brand Story
avoidFor: Dense admin tools and technical documentation
firstViewport: Brand name, emotional promise, image-led product or lifestyle signal, simple CTA.
layoutRules: Use real or generated visual assets.
Avoid dashboard chrome unless the product is software-led.
typography: Expressive heading paired with clear body and CTA text.
risks: Needs strong imagery; otherwise it feels like a generic landing page.

name: Portfolio Studio Grid
firstViewport: Name or studio mark, sharp positioning, large work preview, restrained navigation.
layoutRules: Let work samples carry the page.
Use consistent image ratios and editorial spacing.
typography: Confident editorial type with disciplined captions and metadata.
risks: Can obscure conversion if contact path is too subtle.

name: Commerce Premium Catalog
firstViewport: Product-first hero, price or collection signal, clear purchase or browse path.
layoutRules: Use real product imagery and stable product grids.
Do not hide purchase affordances behind decorative layouts.
typography: Retail-readable hierarchy with crisp product metadata.
risks: Needs product assets; placeholders will break the premium feel.

name: AI Lab Research Notebook
avoidFor: Consumer shopping and simple local business sites
firstViewport: Research statement, benchmark or diagram, model/product artifact, direct technical CTA.
layoutRules: Use figures, tables, code, and benchmark blocks.
Keep experimental visuals readable and grounded.
typography: Research-paper clarity with modern product polish.
risks: Can feel academic if product action is not visible.

name: Fintech Precision Trust
firstViewport: Financial outcome, trust proof, high-quality chart or report preview, controlled CTA.
layoutRules: Use clear numeric hierarchy and cautious color semantics.
Do not use noisy speculative visuals.
typography: Precise numeric typography and conservative headings.
risks: Needs careful chart semantics; fake finance data looks untrustworthy.

name: Learning Platform Friendly
firstViewport: Outcome-oriented headline, product/course preview, friendly progress or lesson state.
layoutRules: Show progress, lessons, outcomes, and guidance.
Avoid childish visuals unless the audience is children.
typography: Warm but structured hierarchy with readable learning content.
risks: Can become too soft for professional training unless density is tuned.
